(B) It was a 30-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack
(C) It was a 25-litre North Face Trail Hiker Pack
(D) It was a black 25-litre backpack with no visible branding
(E) It was a 20-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack

(B)  
</final_answer>

Reasoning: The FBI stated the suspect was seen wearing a **black, 25-litre Ozark Trail Hiker Pack backpack** in the doorbell camera footage. ([cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/nancy-guthrie-case-fbi-releases-first-description-of-suspect-increases-reward-to-100000-dollars/?utm_source=openai))

Today is February 14, 2026. Nikhil Gupta has pleaded guilty to charges related to a plot to kill a Sikh separatist leader in New York. What is the maximum prison sentence he faces for these crimes?
(A) 10 years
(B) 20 years
(C) 25 years
(D) 30 years
(E) 40 years
explanation: The article states: "Nikhil Gupta, 54, admitted to murder-for-hire, conspiracy to commit murder-for-hire, and conspiracy to commit money laundering. He faces up to 40 years in prison."
model_output:
Based on the available information, Nikhil Gupta faces a **maximum of 40 years** in U.S. federal prison for the charges to which he has pleaded guilty. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/13/nikhil-gupta-assassination-plot-pleads-guilty?utm_source=openai))

Here’s how that total is reached:
- Murder-for-hire: up to 10 years ([dea.gov](https://www.dea.gov/press-releases/2026/02/13/indian-national-pleads-guilty-plotting-assassinate-us-citizen-new-york?utm_source=openai))  
- Conspiracy to commit murder-for-hire: up to 10 years ([justice.gov](https://www.justice.gov/usao-sdny/pr/indian-national-pleads-guilty-plotting-assassinate-us-citizen-new-york-city?utm_source=openai))  
- Conspiracy to commit money laundering: up to 20 years ([dea.gov](https://www.dea.gov/press-releases/2026/02/13/indian-national-pleads-guilty-plotting-assassinate-us-citizen-new-york?utm_source=openai))  

Thus, combining the statutory maximums of those three charges yields a total of **40 years**.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
